CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data TEXT);
SELECT id, data FROM test_data WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO test_data (id, data) SELECT id, data FROM another_table WHERE data LIKE 'sample%';
SELECT DISTINCT data FROM test_data WHERE data IN (SELECT data FROM test_data WHERE id < 10);
SELECT t1.id, t1.data FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.data = t2.data WHERE t1.id <> t2.id;
CREATE VIEW view_data AS SELECT id, data FROM test_data WHERE id % 2 = 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM view_data WHERE data LIKE 'example%';
DELETE FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM view_data WHERE data IS NULL);
SELECT t1.id, t2.data FROM test_data t1 LEFT JOIN view_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.data IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE data LIKE '%test%' AND id IN (SELECT id FROM another_table WHERE condition);
